turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having this crampy pain in my lower abdomen, mostly on the left side, and it's really been bothering me. I've also had diarrhea nonstop for about two weeks now – 8 to 10 times a day, and it's just loose or watery with an urgency that's hard to deal with.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy pain in my lower abdomen, especially on the left side, is pretty constant. It's what's really been driving me crazy – it's like a sharp, stabbing feeling that doesn't go away. The diarrhea is just a huge inconvenience on top of that.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about two weeks ago, I'd say. I was working long hours and eating takeout for lunch most days, but I've been doing that for years, so I'm not sure if it's related to anything specific.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens every time I have a bowel movement now. So, 8-10 times a day, it feels like my abdomen is cramping right before I go.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's an 8 out of 10 - the pain is constant and sharp, and the diarrhea is just really inconvenient and stressful. It's affecting my work performance too because I'm always worried about being close to a bathroom and having to rush off.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: The fatigue is pretty bad too - I've been working 60+ hour weeks, but this has taken it to another level. Even after I get some rest, I still feel exhausted and just want to lie down.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: It gets worse when I'm at work, especially during meetings or presentations - the urgency of having to rush to the bathroom is always there, which makes me really anxious. And even if I make it through a meeting without an issue, the fatigue and stress from worrying about it wears off on me throughout the day.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: No, I don't experience those symptoms. The main thing that's been really bothering me is the crampy pain and the constant diarrhea.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: No, it doesn't spread to anywhere else. It's pretty localized to my lower left abdomen. The cramping is what's really been driving me crazy – it's like a sharp, stabbing feeling that's always there.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any swelling in my legs or feet. To be honest, I'm more concerned about getting some relief from this crampy pain and diarrhea than anything else right now – it's been really disrupting my life.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been taking Imodium, loperamide, for the past two weeks to try and control the diarrhea. It hasn't really helped much, though - I still have 8-10 bowel movements a day, and it's just a huge inconvenience on top of the crampy pain.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: My father has type 2 diabetes. My mother is healthy, though. I don't have any other known medical conditions that I'm aware of. To be honest, I haven't been to a doctor in over two years, so I'm not really sure what my bloodwork or test results are like currently.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I'm not sure... I don't think I've had it checked recently. To be honest, I haven't been to a doctor in over two years, so I'm not really sure what my medical history looks like right now. (pauses) But can we focus on the symptoms I'm experiencing and see if you can help me figure out what's going on?

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I drink 2-3 beers most weeknights, but over the past month, I've increased it to 4-5 per night because of the stress from my symptoms and work.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient: To be honest, my diet has been pretty poor lately due to my long hours at work. I've mostly been eating takeout for lunch most days of the week, which isn't great. And in terms of exercise, I don't really have time for it anymore with my 60+ hour workweeks.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, not that I'm aware of. My father has type 2 diabetes, like I mentioned earlier. But no one in my family comes to mind with heart problems. (pauses) To be honest, I'm more concerned about getting some relief from this crampy pain and diarrhea than anything else right now – it's been really disrupting my life.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, but that's about it. I'm not sure if there's any connection to what I'm experiencing now, but I've been worried that maybe it could be something similar. Can we talk more about what might be causing my symptoms?

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over 2 years since I've seen a doctor, no recent bloodwork or tests have been done. To be honest, I'm starting to get really worried about what might be going on with my body and I just want some relief from this crampy pain and diarrhea.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: I don't think so. I've been taking Imodium, loperamide, for the past two weeks without any issues, but that's about it in terms of medication. Like I said, it hasn't really helped with my symptoms, though.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: To be honest, I've been so focused on trying to get relief from these crampy pains and diarrhea that I haven't really thought about other aspects of my health. But now that you mention it, I have lost a bit of weight unintentionally over the past few weeks – maybe around 4 kg (9 lbs) in total? And my fatigue is definitely worse than usual, which is affecting my work performance and just overall quality of life.
